issue_body:
Date math involving nulls fails type resolution.  It looks to me like this happens because we get into `DateTimeArithmeticOperation#resolveType` with one of the types being `null`.  This is different than, for example, `double + null`, which does not end up with a `null` type for the `null` value, presumably because there's only one type it could be.  `datetime + null`, on the other hand, could be either `datetime + period` or `datetime + duration`.  Both of those have `datetime` as the result, but it gets worse with `period + null`, which could have either a `period` or a `datetime`, depending on the type of the `null`

Currently, we expect (and enforce through testing) that expressions involving nulls should still be able to resolve their types.  For now, I'll disable the null checks for adding datetimes, which is unfortunately a somewhat manual process.  Essentially, to enable these tests, add the `anyNullIsNull` call to the test case generation *after* the date cases are in the supplier list.

Relates to https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/102830
commit_message:
ESQL: allow `null` in date math (#103610)

This fixes `null`'s handling in date math. So far the `null` (of type
`NULL`) has been rejected by the type resolution. This is now allowed
through, leading to a `null` result, inline with the other types.

Fixes #103085.

commit_message:
Add IndexVersion constant for 8.13 (#103096)

This commit adds a dummy IndexVersion constant for 8.13. It is needed
for CCR tests which expect a different index version between current and
previous releases.

commit_message:
Use LogDocMergePolicy in CompositeAggregatorTests (#103174) (#105494)

* Use LogDocMergePolicy in CompositeAggregatorTests

The latest lucene upgrade adds a new merge policy that reverse the
order of the documents. To avoid randomisation we hardcode the merge
policy to LogDocMergePolicy.

This has also been applied to NestedAggregatorTests, so the logic is
moved to AggregatorTestCase to be available to all agg tests.

Fixes #103105

* make policy optional

* update comment

* suggested updates

Co-authored-by: Kostas Krikellas <131142368+kkrik-es@users.noreply.github.com>

commit_message:
Deep copy term returned from ordinal lookup (#103167)

We should perform a deep copy of the term returned from the ordinal 
lookup before passing it to the constant block since that BytesRef may
be reused in subsequent lookups.

Closes #103106

issue_title: [Transform] Date fields are not output as ISO strings when `deduce_mappings==false`
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

main

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

n/a

### Problem Description

When `deduce_mappings` is set to `false`, the date fields in the transform's group-by section are written to destination index as epoch millis, despite `dates_as_epoch_millis` being `false`.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create an `unattended` transform with `deduce_mappings` set to `false`. Transform's destination index should not exist yet.
2. Start the transform.
3. Observe `date` fields being written to dest index as `long`s

### Logs (if relevant)

_No response_

commit_message:
Fix owningBlockFactory for VectorBlock (#103157)

We need to override the BlockFactory method in the VectorBlock class to 
ensure it returns the latest owning block factory. This is mainly for
testing. I will then work on a follow-up PR to clarify the ownership of 
the block factory in the Block/Vector.

Closes #103119

commit_message:
ESQL: Update ToDoubleTests's random string conversion checks (#103462)

This updates the checks for warnings triggered by parsing random
strings. Besides some UTF8 issues, there are more ways the parsing can
fail than tested before.

Fixes #103127

commit_message:
Reduce concurrent level in ManyShardsIT (#103159)

This test failed during testing with a single CPU. To prevent test 
failure in such cases, we should lower the concurrency level, ensuring
that it doesn't spawn more than 1000 tasks.

Closes #103128

issue_body:
Reproduces more or less deterministically on https://demo.elastic.co

ES|QL: Fix NPE on single value detection (#103150)

Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/103141

Fix NullPointerException in detection of single value fields in
SearchStats.

commit_message:
[ML] Detect timeout when waiting for download task (#103197)

A list tasks timeout indicates the task exists and is in progress. 
Interpreting the timeout as the task not existing meant the download 
check would incorrectly assume the download had completed.

commit_message:
Fix and simplify testTargetThrottling (#103397)

There seems to be two potential issues that can cause failure: 1. If
the`INDICES_RECOVERY_MAX_BYTES_PER_SEC_SETTING` on nodeA is too small,
then we might choose a chunk size on nodeB that can cause throttling on
nodeA.  2. At the end when we remove the throttling limits, it is
possible that first nodeB gets unthrottled which can cause nodeA
throttling recovery, it seems. 

Closes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/103204

commit_message:
Fix the assertion in MetricApmIT (#104420) (#105620)

The test should print out the assertions that were not matched if it has not managed to finish within 30s
relates #103286

issue_title: Highlighting can take 50% to 99% of search time
commit_message:
Fix performance bug in `SourceConfirmedTextQuery#matches` (#105930)

This change ensures that the matches implementation of the `SourceConfirmedTextQuery` only checks the current document instead of calling advance on the two phase iterator. The latter tries to find the first doc that matches the query instead of restricting the search to the current doc. This can lead to abnormally slow highlighting if the query is very restrictive and the highlight is done on a non-matching document.

Closes #103298

issue_title: ES|QL: KEEP * before MV_EXPAND fails resolution
issue_body:
```
from employees | keep * | mv_expand first_name
```

throws UnresolvedException:

```
org.elasticsearch.xpack.ql.capabilities.UnresolvedException: Invalid call to attribute on an unresolved object ?*
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.ql.expression.UnresolvedNamedExpression.toAttribute(UnresolvedNamedExpression.java:44)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.ql.expression.Expressions.asAttributes(Expressions.java:40)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.ql.plan.logical.Project.output(Project.java:62)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.plan.logical.MvExpand.<init>(MvExpand.java:31)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.parser.LogicalPlanBuilder.lambda$visitMvExpandCommand$3(LogicalPlanBuilder.java:154)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.parser.LogicalPlanBuilder.visitCompositeQuery(LogicalPlanBuilder.java:89)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.parser.LogicalPlanBuilder.visitCompositeQuery(LogicalPlanBuilder.java:66)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.parser.EsqlBaseParser$CompositeQueryContext.accept(EsqlBaseParser.java:232)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.ql.parser.ParserUtils.typedParsing(ParserUtils.java:51)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.parser.LogicalPlanBuilder.plan(LogicalPlanBuilder.java:73)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.parser.LogicalPlanBuilder.visitSingleStatement(LogicalPlanBuilder.java:82)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.parser.LogicalPlanBuilder.visitSingleStatement(LogicalPlanBuilder.java:66)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.parser.EsqlBaseParser$SingleStatementContext.accept(EsqlBaseParser.java:169)
```

The problem is that MV_EXPAND tries to pre-calculate the output too early, when the resolution did not happen yet.

commit_message:
ESQL: Fix SingleValueQueryTests Warnings checks (#103436) (#104056)

This fixes the way we check for Warnings in SingleValueQueryTests, to
account for tests that require full data set scans outside the range the
inner query filters for.

Fixes #102997, #103360.

commit_message: Fix failing test in release mode (#103883)

issue_title: [ML] Gracefully stopping a deployment times out while closing the pytorch process
commit_message:
[ML] Wait for the model results on graceful shutdown (#103591)

#103414 shows a timeout exception when the pytorch_inference
process is gracefully shutdown. The timeout occurs waiting for
the log stream to close but that will not happen until the process
has finished processing the queued requests.

This change waits for that work to complete after closing the
input stream but before waiting on the log stream to close.

The error in #103414 does not mean that the process has not
stopped properly rather it is a short timeout as at that point it
was expected that all the work is complete but this was not the
case. The process would continue to shutdown properly.

Closes #103414

commit_message: AwaitsFix for #103439

issue_title: ESQL: `"lt[e]": "now"` in a range query `filter` erroneously returns nothing
commit_message:
Fix now in millis for ESQL search contexts (#103474)

Currently, ESQL search contexts do not have the correct actual 'now' in 
milliseconds. Ideally, we should use a consistent 'now' in millis for
all nodes participating in the execution of a search request. I will
follow up with a PR to deserialize that value between nodes for version
8.13 and later.

Closes #103455

commit_message:
Re-enable HeapAttackIT (#104107)

This PR enables ESQL heap attack tests. I have run this suite over 500 
iterations with different hardware configurations for the last two days,
and all have been successful with the changes in #104159.

Additionally, this PR adds an action that can trigger OOM to generate a 
heap dump if a test takes more than 5 minutes. I've seen cases
(previously with our CI) where the test didn't result in OOM but was
taking too long. Having the ability to inspect the heap in such cases
would be beneficial.

Closes #103527
Closes #100678

issue_title: ESQL:  IsNull/IsNotNull and other queries are not pushed down
issue_body:
### Description

The local physical planner is too strict and ends up not pushing down filters such as is null/is not null which heavily impact performance.
commit_message:
ESQL: Improve pushdown of certain filters (#103538)

The check of what filters can be pushed down was too strict leading to
 some queries to be evaluated outside the storage. This PR addresses the
 issue in particular is not null and is null filters.

Fix #103536

commit_message:
[ML] Fix JobsAndModelsIT (#137084)

This PR removes assertion assertRecentLastTaskStateChangeTime at the end of the test.

This assertion is not required for the core purpose of the test to verify that:

Jobs and models don't get allocated to the same node when they don't fit
Both eventually get allocated to different nodes when capacity allows
At the same time, it introduce the dependency on how busy the CI is when running the test.

issue_title: EQL: NPE on missing events query
issue_body:
A query like 

```
sequence by user.name with maxspan=90m
![ authentication where ... ]
[ authentication where ...]
![ authentication where ... ]
[ authentication where ... ]
![ authentication where ... ]
```

can result in a NullPointerException

```
java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "org.elasticsearch.xpack.eql.execution.sequence.Match.ordinal()" because the return value of "org.elasticsearch.xpack.eql.execution.sequence.Sequence.matchAt(int)" is null
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.eql.execution.sequence.TumblingWindow.prepareQueryForMissingEvents(TumblingWindow.java:313)
```

issue_title: [ES|QL] is_finite and is_infinite only accepts double types
issue_body: ### Elasticsearch Version 8.13 ### Installed Plugins _No response_ ### Java Version _bundled_ ### OS Version MacOS ### Problem Description The ES|QL math functions `is_finite` and `is_infinite` only accepts `double` values and errors on `integer` or other types: Also, this thing is not documented: https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/esql-functions-operators.html#esql-is_infinite ### Steps to Reproduce See above ### Logs (if relevant) _No response_
commit_message:
ESQL: Improve docs for the floating points is_xxx() funtions (#103691)

Improve the docs for is_nan, is_finite, is_infinite functions.
This also adjusts the CamelCase to snake_case conversion, to not
consider the last capital letter (like in `IsNaN`).

commit_message:
When summing cartesian centroids, use floats to reduce differences to test code (#103730)

* When summing centroids, the error accumulates, so compensate

In this case we noticed very rare failures, usually less than 1/1000 test runs failed, but in the two cases we detected, the failure occurred when the last geometry caused the centroid summation to switch signs, which also lead to much smaller numbers (changing sign means the last value must be a very large value opposite of the previous sum). These small numbers could not reliably be used for relative error calculation because the error is based on the complete history, not the final value.

* Revert "When summing centroids, the error accumulates, so compensate"

This reverts commit 624084b92672cb5e707d6c9f63e9317d72b009d8.

* When summing cartesian centroids, use (float) to reduce test differences

The cartesian geometries are stored in Lucene using float space, not doubles, but the test data was entirely in doubles. This leads to accumulating differences as centroid and centroids of centroids are calculated, so we rarely (1/10000) get test failures as a result. This fix just changes the centroid of centroids calculation to use floats to reduce those differences.

commit_message:
Re-enable MultiClusterSpecIT (#104023)

This PR re-enables the multi clusters spec tests after we 
fixed an unreleased bug in ComputeService.

Closes #103737

commit_message:
Use cancel reason in ESQL (#103824)

ComputeService in ESQL should use the cancel reason
when cancelling its exchanges.

Closes #103746

commit_message:
Harden exchange source handler (#104003)

There is a bug introduced when we added support for cross clusters query 
in ESQL. If there is a long pause before the ComputeService sends
cluster requests to remote clusters, we risk finishing the exchange
source without linking remote sinks of the remote clusters. I can
reproduce this by inserting a long pause, but I couldn't write a useful
test unless we add a tracer and provide callbacks so that we can pause
in tests. This PR hardens and simplifies the exchange source handler. I
will review and simplify the exchange service as the distributed
execution has evolved, but we haven't simplified the exchange service
much accordingly.

Closes #103747
Closes #103749

commit_message:
Fix test logic for immediate results in EsqlAsyncActionIT (#103761)

This commit fixes a a race in the test logic. The request can return results immediately and also an async execution id if keepOnCompletion is true. The test is updated to reflect this.

commit_message:
Harden exchange source handler (#104003)

There is a bug introduced when we added support for cross clusters query 
in ESQL. If there is a long pause before the ComputeService sends
cluster requests to remote clusters, we risk finishing the exchange
source without linking remote sinks of the remote clusters. I can
reproduce this by inserting a long pause, but I couldn't write a useful
test unless we add a tracer and provide callbacks so that we can pause
in tests. This PR hardens and simplifies the exchange source handler. I
will review and simplify the exchange service as the distributed
execution has evolved, but we haven't simplified the exchange service
much accordingly.

Closes #103747
Closes #103749

Fix memory tracking in enrich query operator (#103775)

Closes #103764

issue_body:
Queries with duplicated aggregations don't work in a mixed cluster between 8.11 and 8.12+. This is can reproduced as follow:

1. Set up a mixed cluster with two nodes: one 8.11.x node and one 8.12+ node
2. Index data into the new node only
3. Run this query against the old node
	```
	from employees | stats m = median(salary_change), p50 = percentile(salary_change, 50)
	```
4. ESQL failed with this exception:

```
Caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.planner.Layout$ChannelAndType.channel()" because "input" is null
    at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.planner.LocalExecutionPlanner.planProject(LocalExecutionPlanner.java:536)
    at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.planner.LocalExecutionPlanner.plan(LocalExecutionPlanner.java:199)
    at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.planner.LocalExecutionPlanner.planExchangeSink(LocalExecutionPlanner.java:325)
    at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.planner.LocalExecutionPlanner.plan(LocalExecutionPlanner.java:229)
    at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.planner.LocalExecutionPlanner.plan(LocalExecutionPlanner.java:170)
    at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.plugin.ComputeService.runCompute(ComputeService.java:404)
    at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.plugin.ComputeService$DataNodeRequestHandler.lambda$messageReceived$5(ComputeService.java:588)
    at org.elasticsearch.action.ActionListener$2.onResponse(ActionListener.java:169)
    at org.elasticsearch.action.ActionListener.completeWith(ActionListener.java:274)
    at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.plugin.ComputeService.lambda$acquireSearchContexts$21(ComputeService.java:454)
    at org.elasticsearch.index.shard.IndexShard.ensureShardSearchActive(IndexShard.java:3967)
    at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.plugin.ComputeService.acquireSearchContexts(ComputeService.java:452)
    at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.plugin.ComputeService$DataNodeRequestHandler.messageReceived(ComputeService.java:581)
    at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.plugin.ComputeService$DataNodeRequestHandler.messageReceived(ComputeService.java:572)
    at org.elasticsearch.transport.RequestHandlerRegistry.processMessageReceived(RequestHandlerRegistry.java:75)
    at org.elasticsearch.transport.InboundHandler.doHandleRequest(InboundHandler.java:288)
    at org.elasticsearch.transport.InboundHandler$1.doRun(InboundHandler.java:301)
    at org.elasticsearch.common.util.concurrent.ThreadContext$ContextPreservingAbstractRunnable.doRun(ThreadContext.java:984)
    at org.elasticsearch.common.util.concurrent.AbstractRunnable.run(AbstractRunnable.java:26)
    at org.elasticsearch.common.util.concurrent.TimedRunnable.doRun(TimedRunnable.java:33)
    at org.elasticsearch.common.util.concurrent.ThreadContext$ContextPreservingAbstractRunnable.doRun(ThreadContext.java:984)
    at org.elasticsearch.common.util.concurrent.AbstractRunnable.run(AbstractRunnable.java:26)
    at java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1144)
    at java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:642)
    at java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1583)
```

The issue went unnoticed due to a bug in the mixed-cluster QA module (see #103758). Although I haven't investigated the details, I believe the introduction of `ReplaceDuplicateAggWithEval` in #100642 is causing the disparity between these versions. I think we should disable this rule in both 8.12 and 8.13, and reintroduce it later.
commit_message:
Re-enable spec tests in mixed cluster (#103832)

This pull request re-enables ESQL spec tests in a mixed cluster by 
implementing these changes:

1. Skipping spec tests with repeated aggregations in a mixed cluster
between versions 8.11 and 8.12+ due to the impact of the
ReplaceDuplicateAggWithEval rule.

2. Avoiding access to the block factory in the InMapper constructor to
prevent AssertionError when the driver context is ThrowingContextDriver.

Closes #103765

commit_message:
ESQL: Fix memory tests (#104082)

This improves the memory tests of ESQL by removing the hard memory
limits, instead calculating "how much memory is too much" on the
fly. We do a binary search on the operation itself, looking for
the first value that'll throw.

Closes #103789

commit_message:
[ML] Fix step change test

We can get "unlucky" in this test and produce data which is more consistent with
a trend before or after the change point. We are also now more sensitive to better
data fit for complex hypotheses.

As long as we get the change at the correct point and either a step or trend change
that's all we really care about; I've updated the test accordingly.

Fixes #103790.

commit_message:
[Test] Disable rare actions in RepoAnalysisFailureIT (#104109)

Rare actions are disabled to improve CI stability.

Resolves: #103791

issue_title: ESQL: LIKE/RLIKE not consistent in they way the manage MV
issue_body:
All ESQL binary operators perform exact checks on multi-values, ie. `multi_val == single_val` always returns false, even if `single_val` is one of the values in `multi_val`.

This is true also for `LIKE` and `RLIKE`, eg. the following queries return no results

```
row a = ["foo", "bar"] | where a == "foo"
row a = ["foo", "bar"] | where a like "fo*"
```

There is one case that does not seem to be covered properly though, that is when LIKE/RLIKE operators are pushed down to Lucene, eg. 

```
from employees | where jobs LIKE "Account*"
```

This query also incorrectly returns records that have a multi-value `jobs` value
commit_message: ESQL: Add single value checks on LIKE/RLIKE pushdown (#103807)

[ML] Unmute model deployment BWC tests (#105728)

The failure of #103808 might be fixed by the upgrade to PyTorch 2.1.2.
It's not good that we have this entire suite muted, so I'll try
unmuting for a bit and see if we get any repeat failures.
